title: pilosella officinarum subsp. micradenia
intro: Pilosella officinarum subsp. micradenia, also known as False Sow Thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Pilosella officinarum subsp. micradenia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
